summary: Living Identity Phase 2 (Re-Attestation Protocol) tested end-to-end. Backend: 19 pytest tests covering /authorize-partner, /public-challenge/{token} (POST + GET /info), token exhaustion, _mask_email helper, WS emit code paths, and regression for /me, /history, /admin/drift, /admin/billing, /score, escrow templates — all 19 pass (2 had transient 502 from preview ingress on first run, deterministically pass on retry). Frontend: /identity/challenge/{token} public page renders Verify Identity card with masked email (a***n@notarychain.com), uses_remaining=5, expiry date, no-auth required; /identity/challenge/INVALID shows the invalid-token-state correctly; admin /identity dashboard 'Issue Challenge QR' button opens the ChallengeQRModal, 'Generate QR Code' renders an SVG QR + shareable URL (https://...emergentagent.com/identity/challenge/{token}) with copy button; modal footer correctly shows 'Expires in 7 days · 3 uses max'. WebSocket emit paths for living_identity_drift_detected and living_identity_score_changed verified by source inspection in /_do_refresh.

minor.issue: Real biometric-image POST takes 30-90s (Hedera mainnet + S3 SSE-S3 + GPT-5.2 Vision). Preview ingress occasionally returns 502 on the first cold call; subsequent calls are stable. Not a code bug — recommend adding a hint in client UI ('this may take 30-60 seconds').

issues: Pre-existing from iteration 88 — tier band labels '90 verified', '70 watch', '40 challenged' overlap gridlines slightly. Not introduced by Phase 2.

critical_code_review_comments: living_identity_routes.py:625-629 — expiration check inside try/except has a `raise HTTPException(...)` swallowed by the bare `except Exception: pass`. Same bug as flagged in iteration 88 for the partner-challenge route, now repeated in /public-challenge/{token}. An expired-token whose timestamp is malformed would be treated as valid. Fix: re-raise HTTPException explicitly: `except HTTPException: raise; except Exception: pass`.

living_identity_routes.py:630-631 — uses_count check is read-then-write (line 630 reads, line 663-664 increments). Not atomic — concurrent QR scans could exceed max_uses by 1. Use an atomic findOneAndUpdate with `$inc` guarded by `{uses_count: {$lt: max_uses}}` to make it race-safe. Token-exhaustion test still passes today because tests are sequential.

PublicChallenge.jsx — no rate-limit / captcha on the public POST. Anyone with a token URL can spam biometric submissions until uses_count is exhausted. Consider IP-based rate limit on /public-challenge/{token} (e.g., 5/min/IP) before public launch.
living_identity_routes.py:706-712 — _mask_email returns '—' for empty/no-@; not exported, only used internally. OK. Unit-tested.

PublicChallenge.jsx — public token URL is logged in the QR modal as plain text and passed via path param. If a user accidentally copies the URL into a chat/email, anyone who sees it can challenge identity. Consider showing a warning 'Treat this link like a password' in the modal.

LivingIdentity.jsx WS subscription (useWS) — page subscribes via wss://.../api/ws/global which fails closed in the test browser ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'). The subscription is best-effort and silently no-ops, so functionality still works via polling on /me. WS reconnect retry logic should be verified for production-grade real-time alerts.

success_rate.backend: 100% (19/19 — 2 flaky on cold ingress, pass on retry)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (public page intro + invalid + dashboard QR modal + SVG render + shareable URL + copy)

test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! (existing identity, used to issue tokens). Working public-challenge tokens generated during this run: acde53b0dbe343b39fca8c24c18aa70c (4 uses left), 9ede5011d35d459aa99edf015a97ad93 (3 uses left).
seed_data_creation: Per pytest run: ~3 identity_partner_authorizations (admin → admin partner), ~3 identity_challenges (via=public_qr), ~3 identity_snapshots, $0 billed (public flow is free per design). Hedera mainnet seals on topic 0.0.10373605 from this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_living_identity_phase2.py (19 tests, ~75s runtime). Phase 1 tests at test_living_identity.py still pass. Frontend public page at /identity/challenge/:token is fully data-testid-instrumented (public-challenge-page, invalid-token-state, intro-state, capturing-state, submitting-state, result-state, challenger-name-input, start-challenge-btn, snap-btn, retake-btn, submit-btn, hedera-seal-link). Dashboard QR modal: issue-challenge-qr-btn, challenge-qr-modal, qr-partner-name-input, qr-duration-input, qr-max-uses-input, issue-qr-token-btn, qr-display, copy-qr-url-btn. WebSocket warning ('WebSocket is closed before the connection is established') is benign — test browser does not maintain WS, but the emit code paths in routes/living_identity_routes.py:357-368 and :387-400 are correctly wired into _do_refresh for both drift-detected and score-changed events. Two backend tests (test_public_challenge_success_no_auth, test_uses_remaining_decremented) may show transient 502 on cold ingress — re-run in isolation to confirm.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The two backend test failures during the bulk pytest run were caused by preview-environment ingress 502 on a 30-90s real-Hedera+S3+GPT-5.2 biometric submission (cold path). Re-ran the same two tests in isolation: both passed in 10s. Recommend bumping ingress timeout or adding a retry helper to the test suite; not a product bug.
